heading: Authority to extend preferences
label: 2461 Authority to extend preferences
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 2461, , , ; , , , related to authority to extend preferences, prior to the general amendment of this subchapter by .Pub. L. 93–618, title V, § 50188 Stat. 2066Pub. L. 98–573, title V, § 50298 Stat. 3018Pub. L. 104–188Jan. 3, 1975Oct. 30, 1984

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–188, title I, § 1953110 Stat. 1926Aug. 20, 1996“(a).—In GeneralThe amendments made by this subtitle [subtitle J (§§ 1951–1954) of title I of , enacting this subchapter, amending sections 2702, 3011, 3202, 3331, and 3551 of this title, , Agriculture, , Commerce and Trade, sections 262p–4p and 2191a of Title 22, Foreign Relations and Intercourse, and , Internal Revenue Code, and enacting provisions set out as a note under ] apply to articles entered on or after .Pub. L. 104–188section 1444–2 of Title 7section 4711 of Title 15section 871 of Title 26section 2101 of this titleOctober 1, 1996“(b)Retroactive Application.—“(1).—General ruleNotwithstanding section 514 of the Tariff Act of 1930 [] or any other provision of law and subject to subsection (c)—19 U.S.C. 1514“(A)any article that was entered—“(i)after , andJuly 31, 1995“(ii)before , andJanuary 1, 1996to which duty-free treatment under title V of the Trade Act of 1974 [this subchapter] would have applied if the entry had been made on , shall be liquidated or reliquidated as free of duty, and the Secretary of the Treasury shall refund any duty paid with respect to such entry, andJuly 31, 1995“(B)any article that was entered—“(i)after , andDecember 31, 1995“(ii)before , andOctober 1, 1996to which duty-free treatment under title V of the Trade Act of 1974 [this subchapter] (as amended by this subtitle) would have applied if the entry had been made on or after , shall be liquidated or reliquidated as free of duty, and the Secretary of the Treasury shall refund any duty paid with respect to such entry.October 1, 1996“(2).—Limitation on refundsNo refund shall be made pursuant to this subsection before .October 1, 1996“(3).—EntryAs used in this subsection, the term ‘entry’ includes a withdrawal from warehouse for consumption.“(c).—RequestsLiquidation or reliquidation may be made under subsection (b) with respect to an entry only if a request therefor is filed with the Customs Service, within 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], that contains sufficient information to enable the Customs Service—Aug. 20, 1996“(1)to locate the entry; or“(2)to reconstruct the entry if it cannot be located.”

children.text: the effect such action will have on furthering the economic development of developing countries through the expansion of their exports;

the extent to which other major developed countries are undertaking a comparable effort to assist developing countries by granting generalized preferences with respect to imports of products of such countries;
the anticipated impact of such action on United States producers of like or directly competitive products; and
the extent of the beneficiary developing country’s competitiveness with respect to eligible articles.
